frame_analyses.description: Initial view of FLS trainer showing the workspace with Penrose drain visible in the center
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean workspace setup, drain properly positioned

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver visible entering the workspace, beginning to engage with suture material
Both instruments now visible, needle being loaded onto needle driver
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination, needle appears properly positioned

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for first pass through the drain at the marked location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady positioning, attempting accurate placement at target marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying sequence, creating loops for surgeon's knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper technique for surgeon's knot initiation with double wrap

frame_analyses.description: Continuing first throw formation, suture being manipulated to create proper knot structure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control of suture tension during knot formation

frame_analyses.description: First throw being tightened down to secure the surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be proper surgeon's knot with adequate tension

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw, switching hands as required by FLS protocol
Second throw being formed and tightened
Third throw formation beginning with another hand switch
Final knot completed and tightened, suture ends visible extending from knot
Task complete with knot secured and no visible gaps in drain closure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Excellent final result with tight knot and complete drain closure

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw as required
Single throw with proper hand switching from throw 1
Single throw with proper hand switching from throw 2

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent closure with no visible gaps, drain securely held by knot

technique_summary: Excellent performance with proper surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching sequence, and secure knot placement. The drain closure is complete with no visible gaps.
improvement_suggestions: Work on slightly faster needle loading to reduce overall time
Practice more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from target marks
strengths: Perfect knot sequence with proper surgeon's knot and hand switching
Excellent drain closure with no gaps
Good bimanual coordination throughout the procedure
